units.name: Pulse
units.prompt: Prioritize healing the lowest HP ally. Position safely behind your sentinel. Use overclock on allies who can do the most with an extra action.

units.name: Deadeye
units.prompt: Position for clear sight lines. Prioritize killing wounded enemies with precision_shot. If forced to move, retreat away from threats. Suppressing_fire to slow rushers.

units.name: Warden
units.prompt: Hold the front line. Position between allies and enemies. Use shield_wall toward incoming threats. Attack adjacent enemies.

units.name: Seer
units.prompt: Scan early to give your team intel on enemy prompts. Target enemy specters with scan to track them after cloak. Use recalibrate on allies who need tactical adjustments. Stay safe at range behind your front line.

units.name: Aegis
units.prompt: Advance toward the nearest enemy. Use shield_wall facing the direction with the most enemies. If an enemy is adjacent, attack them.

units.name: Lifeline
units.prompt: Stay adjacent to the most threatened ally. Use patch on the most wounded teammate. If all healthy, use overclock on your damage dealer.

actions.thinking: Round 1, Deadeye is in scan range at (4,1) dist=3. Scanning reveals their prompt and deals 1dmg - high value intel. I'm safely behind Aegis and Lifeline. Scan Deadeye then hold position.

Deadeye is wounded at 2/3 HP and dist=4. I need to close distance. Moving to (3,1) gets me closer (dist=2 from Deadeye at 4,1). Warden is full HP, no heal needed. Enemies are far. Move toward Deadeye first, then wait since still out of heal range.

All allies are at full HP, so no healing needed. Aegis is the sentinel (damage dealer/tank) at (2,4), adjacent distance 2. I can move to (2,3) to get adjacent to Aegis, then use overclock on Aegis to give them 2 abilities next turn.
Enemies are 3-4 tiles away. Move to (2,2) to advance toward front line and position between allies and enemies. No adjacent enemies to attack or shield.

Seer is in range at dist 3 (≤2 tiles? No, dist=3 exceeds range 2). Check: range 2 means ≤2 tiles. Seer is at dist 3, so out of precision_shot range. No enemies in range 2. I should move north to close distance on Seer while staying at range. Moving to (4,3) puts me dist 1 from Seer at (4,4) — that's adjacent, which blocks precision_shot. Move to (4,2) puts dist=2, within precision_shot range. Then fire precision_shot at Seer.

Warden is closest at dist=2, directly south. Move to (2,3) to get adjacent, then use shield_wall facing S (toward Warden and Pulse beyond). With overclocked I get 2 ability uses — second use: attack Warden who will be adjacent at (2,2).

Deadeye is adjacent at (4,3) and targeted me last turn with precision_shot. I need to move away from Deadeye to avoid being attacked again. Moving to (3,4) puts me 2 tiles from Deadeye and keeps scan range on Warden (dist=3) and Pulse (dist=3). From (3,4) I can scan Warden to get their prompt - valuable intel for the team.

Deadeye is at 2/3 HP (dist=3), Warden is at 7/8 HP (dist=2). Priority is lowest HP ally = Deadeye. But Deadeye is dist=3, need to move adjacent first. Moving to (4,2) puts me at dist=1 from Deadeye at (4,3). From (4,2) I can patch Deadeye. Warden at (2,2) is less critical. Enemies: Seer, Lifeline, Aegis are all to the north/northwest. Moving to (4,2) keeps me away from enemy cluster and adjacent to Deadeye.

Aegis is adjacent at (2,4) and wounded (7/8). Warden is threatening at (2,2) dist=1. I need to heal Aegis who is right next to me. Patch is blocked if I'm adjacent to an enemy - Warden is at (2,2) which is adjacent to me at (2,3). I should move away from Warden first to unblock patch, then heal Aegis. Moving to (1,3) keeps me adjacent to Aegis at (2,4)? No, dist from (1,3) to (2,4) = 2, not adjacent. Move to (1,4) - dist to Aegis (2,4) = 1, adjacent. And Warden at (2,2) dist from (1,4) = sqrt(1+4) > 1, not adjacent. That unblocks patch.
Seer is at dist=2 and healthy - precision_shot does 2dmg from current position without moving. I haven't moved so no penalty. Target Seer to burst them down. Then I can still move or wait.

Warden is 2 tiles south at (2,2), Deadeye is 2 tiles east at (4,4). Orders say advance toward nearest enemy. Both Warden and Deadeye are equidistant at dist=2. Moving to (2,3) closes on Warden and puts me adjacent next turn. After moving I lose Fortify, so use shield_wall facing E toward Deadeye to protect Seer at (3,4) who is wounded.

Aegis is adjacent at (2,3). I'm fortified so I have 50% damage reduction. I should attack Aegis while staying put to keep fortify bonus. No need to move — attack first, then shield_wall north to protect allies from Aegis.

Deadeye is adjacent at (4,4) and just used precision_shot on me last turn — dangerous. Pulse at (4,2) is unscanned and in scan range (dist=3). I should move away from Deadeye first to avoid adjacency attack, then scan Pulse for intel. Moving to (3,3) puts me at dist=2 from Pulse (still in scan range) and away from Deadeye. From (3,3), Pulse at (4,2) is dist=2, still within scan range of 4.

I'm at (4,2) with 4/5 HP. Warden is wounded at (2,2), dist=2. I need to get adjacent to heal. Moving to (3,2) puts me adjacent to Warden at (2,2). Then patch Warden. Seer is wounded nearby at (3,3) but is an enemy. Aegis at (2,3) is dist=3 from me currently. After moving to (3,2), Aegis at (2,3) is dist=2 - not adjacent, so patch won't be blocked. I only have 1 heal remaining so use it on Warden who needs it.

Seer is more wounded (4/7, missing 3HP) but dist=3. Aegis is dist=2, missing 1HP. I have 1 heal left. Move to (2,4) to get adjacent to Aegis at (2,3), then patch Aegis. But Seer is more critically wounded. Can I reach Seer? Seer is at (3,3), dist=3 from me. Valid moves include (3,4) which puts me dist=1 from Seer at (3,3) - adjacent! Move to (3,4), then patch Seer (4/7, more wounded).
Aegis is adjacent at (2,3). I'm fortified with 50% damage reduction. Attack Aegis to deal damage, then stay put to maintain Fortify passive for next turn.

Warden is adjacent at (2,2). Orders say attack adjacent enemies. I'm fortified (50% DR) so staying put is fine. Attack Warden, then use shield_wall facing S where allies are clustered — actually enemies are mostly S/E of me. Warden is S of me (at y=2, I'm at y=3 so Warden is S). Pulse is at (3,2) which is SE. Most enemies are to the S. Use shield_wall facing S after attacking.

Lifeline is adjacent at (3,4) - precision_shot is blocked when adjacent to enemy. Seer is at dist=2, in range, not adjacent. I should use precision_shot on Seer (2dmg would kill if wounded, deal heavy damage if healthy). Lifeline is adjacent so use attack on Lifeline instead. Best play: attack Lifeline (adjacent, 1dmg) then precision_shot Seer (2dmg). But precision_shot needs LoS and I haven't moved - I'm at (4,4), Seer at (3,3). Lifeline at (3,4) is adjacent but not blocking LoS to Seer at (3,3). However, precision_shot is blocked when adjacent to enemy - Lifeline is adjacent. So I must move first to break adjacency, then use precision_shot. Move to (5,4) to break adjacency with Lifeline, but then precision_shot deals 1dmg (moved penalty). Alternatively: attack Lifeline first (1dmg), then precision_shot Seer (2dmg, not moved, but Lifeline still adjacent). Actually the warning says precision_shot blocked when adjacent to enemy - so if Lifeline is still at (3,4) after I attack, I'm still adjacent. Better: move to (5,4) first, Seer now at dist=sqrt(4+1)=~2.2 - out of range 2. Move to (4,5): Seer at (3,3) dist=sqrt(1+4)=~2.2 out of range. Move to (5,3): Seer at (3,3) dist=sqrt(4+0)=2, in range, not adjacent to Lifeline at (3,4) dist=sqrt(4+1)>2. Move to (5,3) breaks adjacency, Seer still in range at dist=2, precision_shot deals 1dmg (moved). Or stay and attack Lifeline for 1dmg then can't precision_shot. Best: move to (5,3), precision_shot Seer for 1dmg (moved penalty). Actually (5,4): Lifeline at (3,4) dist=2, Seer at (3,3) dist=sqrt(4+1)~2.24 out of range. Move to (4,5): Lifeline dist=sqrt(1+1)~1.4 still adjacent? No, adjacent means dist=1 exactly. (4,5) to (3,4) = sqrt(1+1)=1.41, not adjacent. Seer at (3,3) dist=sqrt(1+4)=2.24 out of range. Hmm. Stay at (4,4), attack Lifeline (1dmg), but then still adjacent so no precision_shot. Conclusion: move to (5,3), precision_shot Seer (1dmg moved penalty, but removes adjacency issue).

All three enemies already scanned — no new intel needed. Foresight shows Deadeye will precision_shot (3,3) this turn and I'm at (3,3), so I must move away. Pulse is adjacent at (3,2) — I can scan it but already have its prompt. Priority: survive Deadeye's shot by moving. Moving to (4,3) gets me out of (3,3) and keeps Deadeye at dist 1 which is within scan range but all enemies are already scanned. After moving, Pulse is at (3,2) dist 2 from (4,3) — still in scan range. But all enemies are scanned. I could recalibrate Lifeline at (3,4) — from (4,3) that's dist 2, not adjacent. Best play: move to (4,2) to dodge Deadeye's shot targeting (3,3), then scan Pulse for 1 damage since it's the squishiest support and I can weaken it. From (4,2), Pulse at (3,2) is dist 1 — within scan range of 4. Deadeye at (5,3) is dist 2 — in range. Moving to (4,2) is valid per move list.

Patch is unavailable (0 uses left). I'm at 3/5 HP and adjacent to Seer (enemy). Warden is at (2,2) wounded and adjacent to me. I should use overclock on Warden to give them extra actions, then move to safety. Moving to (2,1) puts me behind Warden and away from enemies.

Patch is unavailable (0 uses remaining). Aegis is wounded at (2,3) dist=2, Seer is wounded at (4,2) dist=3. I should move adjacent to Aegis to support. From (3,4) I can move to (2,4) which is adjacent to Aegis at (2,3). Then use overclock on Aegis to boost their effectiveness.

Aegis is adjacent at (2,3) to my north. I have OVERCLOCKED (2 ability uses) and FORTIFIED (didn't move last turn, 50% dmg reduction). Attack Aegis, then shield_wall North to block Lifeline at (2,4) from hitting me or allies. Staying put preserves Fortify.

Seer is wounded and only 2 tiles away at (4,2) - perfect range for precision_shot for 2 damage. I haven't moved so full damage applies. Take the shot to eliminate Seer. Then I can move to reposition if needed, but killing Seer is the priority.

Warden is adjacent at (2,2). Orders say attack adjacent enemies and use shield_wall facing direction with most enemies. Warden is N, Pulse is also N (2,1). I'm fortified so staying put preserves 50% damage reduction. Attack Warden first, then shield_wall facing N to block Warden+Pulse. OVERCLOCKED gives 2 ability uses.

I'm at 2HP — critical. Deadeye will precision_shot me again if I stay. Foresight shows Deadeye targeted (4,2) last turn, so I need to move. All enemies already scanned. I should move to safety and recalibrate an ally. Moving to (4,4) gets me away from Deadeye's likely target zone and Warden. Aegis is wounded at (2,3) — Lifeline is already overclocking them so they're covered. I'll move north to (4,4) to break line of sight pressure, then recalibrate Lifeline if adjacent — but Lifeline is at (2,4), too far. Better to recalibrate Aegis but they're at (2,3), also too far. No adjacent allies to recalibrate. I'll move to (3,3) to stay safe and closer to allies, then wait — or move to (4,4). Best play: move away from Deadeye, then wait. Move to (4,5) to be safe from melee and far from Deadeye's (5,3) position.

Patch is unavailable (0 uses left). Aegis is adjacent at (2,3) and wounded. I should use overclock on Aegis to help them act more. Stay put or reposition to maintain adjacency with Aegis while avoiding Warden at (2,2) who is dist=2. Current position (2,4) keeps me adjacent to Aegis. Use overclock on Aegis, then wait.

Patch is unavailable (0 uses left). I'm at 3/5 HP adjacent to Aegis at dist=2. Warden is at (2,2) adjacent to me and needs healing but I can't patch. I should use overclock on Warden so they can act twice - a sentinel with 2 actions can hold the line better. But Aegis is at (2,3) and Warden is at (2,2), so Warden is adjacent to Aegis - the overclock passive says patch/overclock blocked when adjacent to enemy Vector, not sentinel. I need to check if I'm adjacent to an enemy first. Aegis is at dist=2 from me, so I'm not adjacent. Warden is at (2,2) and I'm at (2,1) - we are adjacent. I can overclock Warden. Then I should move away from danger - move to (1,1) or (0,1) to get safer.
Aegis is adjacent at (2,3) to my north. I'm fortified (50% dmg reduction) and overclocked (2 ability uses). Attack Aegis first, then shield_wall north to block Lifeline at (2,4) from pushing through.

Seer is wounded at dist=2, within precision_shot range. I haven't moved, so precision_shot deals full 2dmg — enough to eliminate a wounded target. Take the shot at Seer first. Then I can move to reposition if needed, but Seer is the priority kill.

Warden is adjacent at (2,2). I have 2 ability uses (overclocked) and fortified. Orders say attack adjacent enemy and use shield_wall facing direction with most enemies. Warden is S of me, Deadeye and Pulse are also roughly S/SW. Attack Warden first, then shield_wall facing S to cover that direction.

Patch is unavailable (0 uses left). Aegis at (2,3) is critically wounded at 2/8 HP and adjacent to me. I should use overclock on Aegis to give them 2 abilities next turn, which could help them survive or reposition. Warden is at (2,2) threatening Aegis. I'm already adjacent to Aegis so I should stay put and use overclock.

I'm at 3/5 HP with no patch uses left. Warden is wounded at (2,2) and is my priority, but I can't heal. I should use overclock on Warden to give them an extra action, then move to a safer position. Warden is at dist=2, so I need to move adjacent first. Moving to (2,1) puts me adjacent to Warden at (2,2). From (2,1) I can overclock Warden. Aegis is at (2,3) which is dist=2 from (2,1) - that would block overclock. Let me check: overclock is blocked when adjacent to enemy Vector, not sentinel. Aegis is a sentinel, so overclock should still work. Actually the rule says 'patch/overclock blocked when adjacent to enemy Vector' - Aegis is a sentinel not a Vector, so overclock is fine. Move to (2,1), then overclock Warden at (2,2).

Warden is adjacent at (2,2) and critical — attack to finish. I have 2 ability uses (overclocked). Enemies are mostly to the south (Pulse at (2,1), Deadeye at (5,2)). After attacking Warden, use shield_wall facing S to block incoming from Pulse/Deadeye direction. Stay put to keep fortify active.

Both enemies are out of range. Aegis is at (2,3) dist=4, Lifeline at (2,4) dist=5. I need range≤2 for precision_shot. Moving to (3,2) gets me to dist=2 from Aegis (critical health) — perfect for precision_shot. I haven't moved yet so precision_shot does full 2dmg, which should finish Aegis.

I'm at 1/8 HP and fortified. Lifeline is at (2,4), distance 2 - not adjacent so can't attack. Pulse is wounded at (2,1) to my south. Moving north to (2,3) closes distance to Lifeline but loses fortify. Better to stay put, keep fortify active, and use shield_wall to protect allies. With overclocked I get 2 ability uses. Shield_wall north to block Lifeline's attacks on me, and shield_wall toward Pulse to protect wounded ally.

Patch is unavailable (0 uses left). Warden at (2,2) is critically wounded at 1/8 HP but I cannot heal. I can use overclock on Warden to give them 2 abilities next turn - they're adjacent at (2,2). I'm at (2,1), Warden is at (2,2) which is adjacent. Use overclock on Warden to maximize their output. Then I should reposition safely - moving to (1,1) puts me further from enemy Lifeline at (2,4).

Patch is unavailable (0 uses left). Pulse is wounded at (1,1), dist 4 - out of range and I can't heal anyway. Lifeline is my adjacent ally to support. I should move toward Pulse to get adjacent and use overclock on Lifeline (the damage dealer). Moving to (1,2) gets me closer to Pulse. Lifeline is at my position area - I need to find Lifeline's position. Order says Lifeline is adjacent (spd2, ally A). I'll move toward Pulse and use overclock on Lifeline if adjacent.

Lifeline is at (1,3), dist=3, in range for precision_shot. I'm at (3,2) moving W, haven't moved yet this turn. I can fire precision_shot first (full 2dmg, no movement penalty), then move if needed. Lifeline is healthy so 2dmg won't kill but damages them significantly. No adjacent enemies so precision_shot is not blocked.

I'm at 3/5 HP with no heals left. Lifeline enemy is 2 tiles away at (1,3) - a threat. Deadeye ally is at full HP so no healing needed. I should move away from Lifeline to avoid being attacked. Moving to (0,0) puts me far from enemy. I can use overclock on Deadeye to boost their damage output, but dist=2 means not adjacent. Let me move to (2,2) - wait, Deadeye is there. Move to (0,1) keeps distance from Lifeline. Actually move to (2,1) to get adjacent to Deadeye for overclock.

Patch is unavailable (0 uses left). Lifeline ally is adjacent ally A at current position - I should stay near threatened allies. Deadeye is the damage dealer at (2,2), dist=2. I can move adjacent to Deadeye to use overclock on them, but Deadeye is an enemy striker. My ally Lifeline is at my position (medic,A). I should use overclock on Lifeline (my damage dealer ally) if they're adjacent, or move to get adjacent to the most threatened ally. Lifeline is listed as order 2 (ally medic A). I need to check adjacency - Lifeline is at my position area. I'll move toward a useful position and use overclock on the best target. Moving to (2,3) puts me adjacent to Deadeye enemy - risky. I'll use overclock on Lifeline if adjacent, otherwise wait.

I'm at (2,2) with 2 HP, overclocked (2 ability uses). Lifeline is at (1,3), dist=2, healthy. I'm a striker with precision_shot doing 2dmg at range≤2. Lifeline has not moved yet this turn (spd2). I should fire twice with precision_shot since I'm overclocked. I haven't moved, so no penalty. Lifeline is at dist=2, in range, no adjacency issue. Two precision shots = 4 total damage, should kill a medic (typically 3HP). Fire twice.
